source: Extracted from CropBinary production DB dump.
overridePolicy: nutrient overrides applied at variant level (matches legacy buildResolvedNutrients, status-ignoring)

canonicals.description: Sulfate of potash (SOP) 0-0-50-18S; low-chloride K source for fertigation and dry blends.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Not classified as hazardous under GHS; avoid dust inhalation; rinse spills with water.

canonicals.description: Chelated Ca stays soluble with phosphates, sulfates, bicarbonates; nitrate-/chloride-free. Stable for fertigation/foliar at pH 5–10. Prevents BER, tip burn, bitter pit by strengthening cell walls/membranes; effective with low transpiration or K/Mg/Na antagonism. Lower EC vs CaCl2 and scaling.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Chelate can mobilize heavy metals in solution; avoid strong acids that can protonate EDTA and release Ca.

canonicals.description: Prilled calcium nitrate double salt for fertigation and dry blends. Grade 15.5-0-0 with 19% Ca; low chloride; stock A only.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Oxidizing salt; may intensify fire. Avoid dust generation and contamination with organic materials. Store cool and dry.

canonicals.description: Chelated iron source that rapidly corrects iron chlorosis, stays soluble in fertigation and foliar sprays, and remains available to plants up to about pH 6.5 for efficient uptake with minimal precipitation losses.

Chloride-free P–K source (0-52-34) that accelerates early rooting, tightens internodes, improves flowering and fruit set, stabilizes solution pH with mild acidifying effect, offers low salt index for sensitive crops, and enables late-stage P–K feeding without added nitrogen for higher sugars/resins.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Generally not classified as hazardous; avoid dust inhalation.

canonicals.description: Highly soluble nitrate fertilizer providing both potassium and nitrate nitrogen. It enhances fruit size, sugar content, color development, and plant strength. Ideal for hydroponics, fertigation, and foliar feeding due to its chloride-free and neutral pH profile.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Oxidizing solid; may intensify fire. Avoid contamination with organic materials.

canonicals.description: Supplies soluble silicon that strengthens cell walls, reduces lodging, improves drought/heat/salinity tolerance, mitigates Al/Mn toxicity in roots, enhances pest and disease tolerance via induced defenses, provides chloride-free K, and elevates solution alkalinity for cleaner lines.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Strongly alkaline; causes severe skin and eye burns; PPE required.

canonicals.description: Highly soluble, chloride-free source of magnesium and sulfur that rapidly corrects Mg/S deficiencies, supports chlorophyll formation and enzyme activity, improves photosynthesis and carbohydrate metabolism, and is suitable for fertigation and foliar feeding due to high purity and full water solubility.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Not classified as hazardous under GHS; dust may cause mild irritation. Store dry and sealed.

canonicals.description: Chloride-free, fast-dissolving boron source with supplemental ammoniacal nitrogen that strengthens cell walls and enhances flowering and pollen tube growth for rapid correction of boron deficiency in fertigation.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Avoid high-pH stock to limit ammonia loss; standard PPE as for ammonium salts.

canonicals.description: Chelated copper source with high stability across pH 3–7 that stays soluble and plant-available in fertigation or foliar use; efficiently corrects Cu deficiency, reduces precipitation losses versus inorganic Cu salts, improves delivery to enzyme systems, and lowers phytotoxicity risk at label rates.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Harmful if swallowed; causes eye/skin irritation; very toxic to aquatic life with long-lasting effects. Avoid release to the environment.

canonicals.description: Chloride-free potassium source that improves fruit size, color, and Brix while supplying sulfur for amino acids and enzyme activity; suited to chloride-sensitive crops and saline soils; stable for fertigation and hydroponics at moderate EC.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Low acute toxicity; dust may irritate eyes/respiratory tract. Store dry; avoid mixing with calcium salts in concentrated stocks.

canonicals.description: High-analysis nitrogen source (46% N) that dissolves rapidly for fertigation and foliar applications, supporting vigorous vegetative growth and efficient nitrogen delivery with relatively low salinity impact compared with many nitrate/ammonium salts.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Generally not GHS-classified; avoid dust inhalation. Keep dry to prevent caking. In concentrated/alkaline stocks, hydrolysis can increase ammonia release; store solutions cool and avoid high pH storage.

canonicals.description: Highly concentrated, cost-effective potassium source that rapidly supports fruit sizing, sugar loading, color development, and overall yield by improving osmotic regulation, stomatal function, and enzyme activation; provides chloride that can enhance turgor, photosynthetic efficiency, and water-use performance in chloride-tolerant crops.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Low hazard; manage dust and avoid eye contact; hygroscopicity can cause caking depending on grade and humidity.

canonicals.description: Supplies essential boron to strengthen cell walls, enhance pollen germination, improve fruit set, and regulate water and sugar transport within the plant.
canonicals.compatibilityNotes: Generally compatible with most fertilizers. Pre-dissolving in warm water is recommended before adding to stock tanks.
canonicals.hazardNotes: May damage fertility or the unborn child (H360FD). Handle with care and avoid inhalation of dust.

canonicals.description: High-P, fully water-soluble starter that provides 12% ammoniacal N and 61% P₂O₅; acidic 1% solution improves phosphorus and micronutrient availability in alkaline waters; chloride-free with low salt index for safe fertigation and foliar use; dissolves rapidly for concentrated stock solutions and early root, bloom, and fruit set response.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Acidic aqueous solution; dust may irritate eyes/respiratory tract; avoid mixing with calcium salts in concentrated form.

canonicals.description: Fast, predictable nitrogen response (nitrate + ammonium) with added calcium and a gentler soil acidification profile than many straight ammonium fertilizers.
canonicals.compatibilityNotes: For concentrated fertigation stocks, keep calcium-containing nitrogen sources separated from phosphates and sulfates to avoid precipitation/scaling. Many CAN grades include limestone/dolomite and are not fully water-soluble; use only if the grade is specified as fully water-soluble for injection systems.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Hygroscopic; avoid caking by keeping sealed and dry. Prevent contamination with fuels/organic materials during storage and handling.

canonicals.description: High-analysis, water-soluble ammonium-N + phosphate source that delivers strong early phosphorus availability for rooting and vigor, supports rapid canopy establishment, and enables high P loading in fertigation stock programs when managed in a calcium-separated system.
canonicals.compatibilityNotes: Keep DAP (phosphate) in a separate stock tank from calcium salts (e.g., calcium nitrate, CaCl2) to prevent insoluble calcium phosphate formation in concentrates. Avoid alkaline stock conditions; high pH increases precipitation risk with hardness and can shift NH4+/NH3 equilibrium. Prefer injecting into flowing water; avoid localized high concentration zones and purge lines when switching between Ca and phosphate feeds.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Dust and concentrated contact can irritate eyes/skin/respiratory tract; use eye protection and dust control/respiratory protection as needed. When heated to decomposition, may release ammonia and phosphorus/nitrogen oxides. Hygroscopic tendency can increase caking; store dry and sealed.

canonicals.description: Provides a high-efficiency phosphorus source with ammonium nitrogen to drive early root development, faster establishment, and improved nutrient uptake, while enabling cost-effective P delivery in granular programs and flexible use in fertigation when calcium is managed separately.
canonicals.compatibilityNotes: Do not concentrate-mix with calcium salts (e.g., calcium nitrate, CaCl2) due to insoluble calcium phosphate precipitation risk. In fertigation, keep phosphate sources in a separate stock tank from calcium; avoid alkaline/high-hardness concentrates and prevent localized high-concentration zones during injection. Flush/purge lines when alternating between Ca and phosphate feeds.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Fertilizer-grade DAP is commonly treated as low acute hazard, but dust can mechanically irritate eyes/skin/respiratory tract; apply standard dust control and PPE. Thermal decomposition can release ammonia and phosphorus/nitrogen oxides.

canonicals.description: Chelated iron (DTPA) providing 11% Fe; stable in solution up to ~pH 7.5.
canonicals.compatibilityNotes: Avoid high-pH stock solutions; dust may be generated. (SDS)
canonicals.hazardNotes: Not classified as hazardous (GHS). Avoid dust inhalation; may cause temporary irritation.

canonicals.description: Manganese + sulfur source; water‑soluble micronutrient.
Zinc + sulfur source; water‑soluble micronutrient.
canonicals.hazardNotes: H302 Harmful if swallowed; H318 Causes serious eye damage (Valudor SDS).

canonicals.description: Delivers a high concentration of nitrogen in both immediate nitrate and sustained ammonium forms to drive rapid vegetative growth and maximize crop yields.
canonicals.compatibilityNotes: Do not mix with limestone or high pH products (releases ammonia gas). Caution when mixing with Potassium Chloride (KCl) in dry blends due to potential chemical instability.
canonicals.hazardNotes: May intensify fire; oxidizer (H272). Causes serious eye irritation (H319).

canonicals.description: A highly soluble source of calcium and chloride that strengthens cell walls, prevents blossom end rot, and improves fruit firmness and shelf life.
canonicals.compatibilityNotes: Incompatible with Sulfates (forms Gypsum) and Phosphates (forms Calcium Phosphate precipitate). Use in a separate stock tank or apply individually.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Causes serious eye irritation (H319). Wash hands thoroughly after handling.

canonicals.description: A highly soluble source of magnesium and chloride, essential for chlorophyll production, enzyme activation, and photosynthesis regulation.
canonicals.compatibilityNotes: Compatible with most soluble fertilizers. Often used in 'Tank B' (Phosphate/Sulfate) stock solutions in hydroponics, as it does not precipitate with Sulfates or Phosphates at standard stock concentrations.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Not classified as hazardous, but may cause mild skin or eye irritation upon contact due to salt concentration.

canonicals.description: Highly stable iron chelate effective in alkaline and calcareous soils (pH 3–10), preventing iron chlorosis and ensuring deep green foliage.
canonicals.compatibilityNotes: Compatible with most fertilizers and pesticides. Do not mix with strong acids or concentrated copper/zinc products which may displace the iron.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Harmful to aquatic life with long lasting effects (H412). Generally non-hazardous to humans under normal use.

canonicals.description: Provides efficient nitrogen and readily available sulfate sulfur to promote vigorous plant growth, protein synthesis, and dark green color.
canonicals.compatibilityNotes: Do not mix with Calcium Nitrate (forms insoluble Calcium Sulfate/Gypsum). Avoid mixing with high pH products (e.g., Potassium Carbonate/Silicate) to prevent Ammonia gas release.
canonicals.hazardNotes: May cause respiratory irritation (H335), skin irritation (H315), and eye irritation (H319).

canonicals.description: Fully water-soluble nitrate magnesium source for fertigation and foliar use; rapidly corrects Mg deficiency to support chlorophyll formation and photosynthesis, and nitrate form promotes efficient magnesium uptake for stronger growth response.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Per Yara SDS: not classified as hazardous under CLP; can create slippery surfaces when wet; keep away from organic materials, oil and grease.

canonicals.description: Copper + sulfur source; water‑soluble micronutrient.
canonicals.hazardNotes: H302 Harmful if swallowed; H318 Causes serious eye damage; H400/H410 very toxic to aquatic life (ChemSupply SDS).

canonicals.description: Molybdenum source; water‑soluble micronutrient.
canonicals.hazardNotes: Toxic to humans and irritating to eyes/skin (ChemicalBook).
